orientation.description: Course runs roughly NW to SE. Hole 1 tee is near center-south, playing NE. Holes route outward and return.
orientation.notes: Sun rises over the hills to the ESE and sets over the Cascades to the WNW. Shadows are longest on early morning and late afternoon rounds.

timeOfDayPresets.name: Early Morning
timeOfDayPresets.description: First tee time. Low sun from the ESE, long shadows, cool temperatures. Dew on greens. Minimal wind -- calmest conditions of the day.
timeOfDayPresets.fogNotes: Light ground fog possible along Yakima River, especially Hole 4 area

timeOfDayPresets.name: Mid-Morning
timeOfDayPresets.description: Sun climbing higher, warming up. Wind typically starting to build. Dew burning off. Best visibility.

timeOfDayPresets.name: Midday
timeOfDayPresets.description: Peak sun, harshest light. Wind at its strongest -- 15-25 mph typical. Semi-arid landscape fully lit. Flat, bright light with minimal shadows.

timeOfDayPresets.name: Afternoon
timeOfDayPresets.description: Sun tracking west, still strong. Wind remains gusty. Warm and dry. Light beginning to warm in color.

timeOfDayPresets.name: Golden Hour
timeOfDayPresets.description: Warm, golden light from the WNW. Dramatic shadows stretch across fairways. Wind typically calming. Best visual conditions for screenshots/photos. Cascade Range silhouette on western horizon.
timeOfDayPresets.notes: Mount Stuart and the Stuart Range may be visible to the NW in clear conditions

timeOfDayPresets.name: Twilight
timeOfDayPresets.description: Post-sunset glow. Deep blue sky with warm horizon. Last holes of the day. Very calm conditions.

weather.notes: Kittitas Valley is one of the driest and windiest areas in WA. Rain shadow of the Cascades. Summer is extremely dry with near-zero precipitation Jun-Aug.

presets.name: Clear & Calm (Rare Morning)
presets.description: Rare calm morning before wind builds. Crystal clear skies, dry air. Best scoring conditions.

presets.name: Typical Kittitas Wind
presets.description: The standard Ellensburg day. Clear to partly cloudy with persistent W-NW wind. This is what most rounds will feel like.

presets.name: Strong Kittitas Wind
presets.description: Common high-wind day. The Kittitas Valley funnels wind through the gap between the Cascades and the Wenatchee Mountains. Significant club selection challenges.
presets.notes: Lenticular clouds over the Cascades are a telltale sign of extreme wind days

presets.name: Partly Cloudy
presets.description: Dramatic Central WA sky with big cumulus clouds. Moderate wind. Temperature comfortable.

presets.name: Overcast with Light Rain
presets.description: Uncommon during golf season but possible in spring and fall. Low clouds, drizzle, reduced wind. Rare enough to be notable.

presets.name: Afternoon Thunderstorm
presets.description: Rare but dramatic summer thunderstorm. Builds over the Cascades and pushes east. Usually brief with gusty winds and possible lightning.

wind.notes: Kittitas Valley is one of the windiest locations in Washington state. Wind is funneled through the valley between the Cascade Range and Saddle Mountains. Wind farm-quality sustained winds are common. Morning calm is the exception, not the rule. Wind typically builds from 9-10 AM and peaks mid-afternoon.

skybox.description: Wide-open Kittitas Valley with mountain backdrops. Stuart Range and Cascade foothills to the NW/W. Manastash Ridge to the SW. Rolling hills to the E. Big sky country with dramatic cloud formations.

horizonFeatures.east: Rolling grassland hills, wind turbines visible on ridgelines
horizonFeatures.south: Manastash Ridge, dry grass and scattered timber
horizonFeatures.northwest: Stuart Range peaks visible on clear days, snow-capped in spring

atmosphericHaze.notes: Wildfire smoke from August fires can significantly reduce visibility and create orange/red skies. Haze values above 0.3 indicate smoke.

seasonalVariations.name: Spring (April-May)
seasonalVariations.description: Course greening up, still some brown patches. Snow possible on mountain backdrops. Cool mornings, mild afternoons. Wind picking up as season progresses.
seasonalVariations.notes: Irrigation system recently activated. Course may have some winter damage spots.

seasonalVariations.name: Early Summer (June)
seasonalVariations.description: Peak course condition. Lush green irrigated turf contrasts with surrounding dry grassland. Long days (15+ hours of daylight). Wind in full effect.
seasonalVariations.notes: Best overall playing conditions. Surrounding hills turning gold.

seasonalVariations.name: Peak Summer (July-August)
seasonalVariations.description: Hot and dry. Irrigated course is a green oasis surrounded by brown/gold landscape. Possible wildfire smoke in August. Firm, fast fairways.
seasonalVariations.notes: Reduced rough height due to heat stress. Course plays fastest. Watch for smoke days in Aug.

seasonalVariations.name: Fall (September-October)
seasonalVariations.description: Cooling temperatures, cottonwoods and willows along the river turn gold and orange. Beautiful backdrop. Wind moderating slightly. Course still in good condition.
seasonalVariations.notes: Fall colors along Yakima River corridor (Hole 4 especially) make for scenic rounds.

layers.description: Constant wind noise -- the signature sound of Ellensburg golf
layers.notes: Wind through grass and trees. Louder and lower-pitched on windy days. Should be the dominant ambient sound.

layers.description: Yakima River flow -- audible on holes 4, 5, and nearby
layers.notes: Gentle river sound, not rapids. More of a steady flow noise. Loudest on Hole 4 near the river bank.

layers.description: Songbirds, meadowlarks, red-winged blackbirds, occasional raptor calls
layers.notes: Western Meadowlark is the WA state bird and common in Kittitas Valley. Red-winged blackbirds near pond (Hole 6) and river.

layers.description: Wind rustling through Ponderosa pine needles
layers.notes: Distinctive whisper/sigh of wind through pine needles. Adds texture to tree-lined holes.
Very faint. Rural highway, low traffic volume.

globalIllumination.notes: Semi-arid climate means intense, direct sunlight with high UV. Dry air produces sharp, well-defined shadows. Very little atmospheric diffusion compared to coastal WA.

teeBox.notes: Well-maintained tee surfaces, typical for irrigated Central WA municipal course

fairway.notes: Irrigated bluegrass fairways. Firm in summer heat, softer in spring. Ball sits up nicely.

primaryRough.notes: First cut rough. Irrigated to maintain green appearance and playability.

secondaryRough.notes: Transition to native vegetation. Brown/gold in summer. Can lose balls here.

green.species: Creeping Bentgrass (Agrostis stolonifera) - likely Penncross or similar
green.notes: Bentgrass greens, typical for PNW inland courses. Speed moderate -- not tour-fast but consistent.

bunkerSand.notes: Typical municipal course bunker sand. Not extremely fine.

nativeAreas.species: Bluebunch wheatgrass, sagebrush, rabbitbrush (beyond course boundary)
nativeAreas.notes: Semi-arid native vegetation visible beyond maintained areas. Provides visual contrast with irrigated turf.

trees.description: Dominant tree species on course. Long needles, distinctive orange-brown bark. Found on multiple holes.
Tall deciduous trees along Yakima River corridor. Turn golden in fall. Produce cotton in late spring.
Found along river banks and near irrigation pond. Drooping branches, dense foliage.

shrubs.description: Native sagebrush visible at course boundaries and unmaintained areas. Aromatic. Silver-green foliage.
shrubs.notes: Adds authentic Central WA character to skybox and course edges.

yakimaRiver.notes: The Yakima River runs along the south/east boundary of the course. Glacially-fed, runs year-round. Cooler water color, not bright blue.

irrigationPond.notes: Used for course irrigation. Likely has some algae growth in summer. Cattails/reeds possible at edges.
